sources.claim: No company name was provided in the user request.

major_developments.title: Insufficient news coverage available
major_developments.summary: No NewsAPI articles were provided that are directly relevant to 'undefined'. Because fewer than three directly relevant articles exist (in fact none), we cannot identify verifiable recent developments from news sources.
major_developments.sales_relevance: Cannot assess — no article data to evaluate potential impacts on sales.

business_priorities: (Inferred — no source) Confirm current strategic priorities and Q-to-date performance to understand immediate needs.
(Inferred — no source) Validate any upcoming product launches, pricing changes, or procurement cycles that would affect buying timelines.

(Inferred — no source) Given lack of external coverage, emphasize our ability to provide confidentiality, proactive intelligence, and a single point of contact to surface hidden needs.
questions_to_ask: What recent internal developments or initiatives should we be aware of that may not be public yet? (Inferred — no source)
Are there upcoming budget cycles, RFPs, or procurement timelines we should align with? (Inferred — no source)
Who are the primary stakeholders and decision-makers for projects related to our solutions? (Inferred — no source)

messages:
System: You are the PRISM Stakeholder Brief Agent.

Your job is to create a concise, sales-relevant brief about the meeting stakeholder.

Use the person's name, role/title, company, meeting type, meeting goal, company research, and news intelligence.

Do not invent personal facts, employment history, education, achievements, opinions, or interests.

If verified personal details are not provided, clearly base insights on the person's role, company context, and meeting goal.

Focus on what the sales rep should understand before the meeting:
- what this stakeholder likely cares about professionally
- how to personalize the conversation
- what objections they may raise
- what questions the rep should ask
- how to communicate effectively

IMPORTANT: For your response to user, you MUST use the `format_final_json_response` tool with your complete answer formatted according to the required schema. Do not attempt to format the JSON manually - always use this tool. Your response will be rejected if it is not properly formatted through this tool. Only use this tool once you are ready to provide your final answer.
Human: Create a stakeholder brief for this meeting.

Person Name: {{ $json.body.personName }}
Role/Title: {{ $json.body.personTitle }}
Company: {{ $json.body.companyName }}
Meeting Type: {{ $json.body.meetingType }}
Meeting Goal: {{ $json.body.meetingGoal }}
Priority: {{ $json.body.priority }}

Company Research:
{
  "company": "Unknown - company name not provided",
  "company_overview": "No specific company name was provided in the request. Please provide the company name or domain to enable accurate business research.",
  "products_services": [],
  "business_model": "Unknown because the company was not specified.",
  "target_customers": [],
  "competitive_positioning": [],
  "pain_points": [
    "Unable to assess company-specific pain points without a company name or website."
  ],
  "growth_opportunities": [],
  "talking_points": [
    "Please share the target company name, website, or ticker symbol so the research can be completed accurately."
  ],
  "questions_to_ask": [
    "Which company should be researched?",
    "Do you have a company website, LinkedIn page, or ticker symbol to disambiguate the organization?",
    "Is this research intended for a specific sales motion, product line, or region?"
  ],
  "sources": [
    {
      "claim": "No company name was provided in the user request.",
      "source": "User request",
      "source_url": "",
      "confidence": "High"
    }
  ]
}

News Intelligence:
{
  "company": "undefined",
  "major_developments": [
    {
      "title": "Insufficient news coverage available",
      "summary": "No NewsAPI articles were provided that are directly relevant to 'undefined'. Because fewer than three directly relevant articles exist (in fact none), we cannot identify verifiable recent developments from news sources.",
      "source": "Input dataset",
      "source_url": "Not available",
      "date": "Not available",
      "sales_relevance": "Cannot assess — no article data to evaluate potential impacts on sales."
    }
  ],
  "positive_signals": [],
  "risk_signals": [],
  "business_priorities": [
    "(Inferred — no source) Confirm current strategic priorities and Q-to-date performance to understand immediate needs.",
    "(Inferred — no source) Validate any upcoming product launches, pricing changes, or procurement cycles that would affect buying timelines."
  ],
  "talking_points": [
    "(Inferred — no source) We have limited public news on your organization; before exploring solutions, can you update us on any recent initiatives or challenges you’re prioritizing?",
    "(Inferred — no source) Given lack of external coverage, emphasize our ability to provide confidentiality, proactive intelligence, and a single point of contact to surface hidden needs."
  ],
  "questions_to_ask": [
    "What recent internal developments or initiatives should we be aware of that may not be public yet? (Inferred — no source)",
    "Are there upcoming budget cycles, RFPs, or procurement timelines we should align with? (Inferred — no source)",
    "Who are the primary stakeholders and decision-makers for projects related to our solutions? (Inferred — no source)"
  ],
  "sources": [
    {
      "title": "No relevant articles provided",
      "url": "Not available",
      "publisher": "NewsAPI input",
      "date": "Not available"
    }
  ]
}

Instructions:
- Keep it concise and useful for a sales rep.
- Separate verified facts from inferred insights.
- If personal details are not available, say so clearly.
- Do not hallucinate biography details.
- Make questions and talking points specific to the stakeholder's role and meeting goal.

output.current_role_context:
Based only on the provided title ({{ $json.body.personTitle }}):
- Likely responsible for driving decisions in functional areas associated with that title (e.g., budgeting, vendor selection, team outcomes, KPI delivery).
- Focus will be on measurable impact (cost savings, revenue uplift, efficiency gains, risk reduction) and on how a solution fits existing processes and technology.
- Will prefer conversations that map proposed capabilities to concrete business metrics and implementation timelines.
output.verified_personal_details_available: No. Only the stakeholder's name, role/title, and company label (as provided above) are available. No verified personal biography, employment history, education, achievements, or interests were supplied.
likely_priorities: Aligning any proposed solution to the stakeholder’s functional KPIs (productivity, revenue, cost, time-to-market depending on role)
Minimizing disruption to current operations and reducing implementation risk
Clear ROI and an accountable timeline for delivery and measurement
Budget predictability and procurement alignment with upcoming cycles
Gaining buy-in from other internal stakeholders and decision-makers
likely_business_concerns: Unclear ROI or weak business case for change
Implementation complexity and integration with existing systems
Budget constraints or misalignment with procurement timelines
Preference for incumbent vendors or internal solutions
Insufficient internal resources to support a pilot or rollout
communication_style_recommendations: Be concise and structured — open with meeting outcomes and desired decisions
Bring two to three data-driven outcomes or case comparisons that map to the stakeholder's KPIs
Use clear timelines and single-page summaries (one-pager or slide) to explain next steps
Anticipate technical or procurement questions; have ROI/back-of-envelope cost models available
personalization_angles: Map capabilities directly to the stakeholder’s functional responsibilities (use their job title as the anchor)
Reference the meeting goal ({{ $json.body.meetingGoal }}) early and confirm it — tailor value props to that goal
If company context is unknown, ask early in the meeting for recent initiatives, current priorities, and any internal constraints to make the conversation relevant
Offer a low-friction next step (pilot, workshop, or a short discovery session) that reduces perceived risk
possible_objections: "We don’t have budget this quarter" — ask about timing and alternative procurement windows
"We already use a vendor/solution for this" — be ready to differentiate on outcomes and TCO
"Integration will be too complex" — provide integration patterns, references, and typical timelines
"We need internal stakeholder buy-in" — identify decision-makers and propose a joint session or brief for approvers
"We don’t see measurable ROI" — show concrete metrics, benchmarks, and proposed measurement plan
stakeholder_specific_questions: Confirm objective: “To make sure I’ve prepared correctly, is your primary goal for this meeting to {{ $json.body.meetingGoal }}?”
KPIs: “Which 1–3 KPIs will define success for this initiative?”
Timeline & budget: “What is your target timeline and is there an allocated budget or approval window we should align to?”
Decision process: “Who else needs to sign off and what criteria do they use?”
Current solutions: “What are you using today to address this problem and what gaps remain?”
Constraints: “Are there technical, regulatory, or procurement constraints we should know about?”
Pilot appetite: “Would a short pilot or proof-of-value be acceptable to validate outcomes?”
Measure & reporting: “How do you prefer progress to be reported and who will consume those reports?”
output.recommended_approach: Before the meeting: send a 1-page agenda that states the meeting goal ({{ $json.body.meetingGoal }}), proposed duration, and desired decisions. Start the meeting by confirming the goal and the stakeholder’s top KPI(s). Use a short, data-focused pitch (3 slides or a one-pager): problem, proposed approach, expected business outcomes, and next steps (pilot or decision). Be prepared with: an ROI/back-of-envelope model, a concise integration summary, and references for similar outcomes. Confirm timeline and decision owners, agree on a small next step (pilot or executive brief), and schedule a follow-up that maps milestones to the stakeholder’s KPIs. Note: Because external company and news data were not provided, open the meeting by asking for recent internal initiatives or constraints to tailor the conversation immediately.

- Will prefer conversations that map proposed capabilities to concrete business metrics and implementation timelines.
